fields.description:
# Re-home CLAUDE.md-native content into authoritative docs

## Why

Five sections of `CLAUDE.md` have NO backing doc — they are the only home for their content:
the `idea` status, session logs (`session_log` type behavior), ticket hierarchy + the
parent-first claim/transition cascade, linking (the six relations + blocking-link hierarchy
promotion), and tags (convergent `TAG_DELTA` add/remove semantics). Before the root file can
be slimmed, this content must land in `docs/` so nothing is lost and both rebar's own
`AGENTS.md` and the client template can point at one source of truth.

## What

Give each of the five native sections an authoritative `docs/` home. Prefer folding into the
closest existing doc where one fits (e.g. session-log behavior near `docs/event-schema.md`;
cascade/linking/tags near `docs/concurrency.md`) and create a single new `docs/ticket-model.md`
for the material that has no natural existing home (idea status + hierarchy + linking + tags),
rather than scattering. Copy content faithfully (no semantic loss), then update
`docs/README.md`'s index to list the new/updated docs.

## Scope

In: `docs/` (new/updated doc files) and `docs/README.md`. Out: editing `CLAUDE.md` itself
(that happens in the AGENTS.md story, which replaces these sections with pointers) and any
runtime/code change.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Each of the five native topics (idea status, session logs, hierarchy + parent-first
  cascade, linking + promotion, tags/TAG_DELTA) has an authoritative doc home with a heading.
  - Proving: `rg -l 'parent-first|TAG_DELTA|hierarchy promotion|status .idea.|session_log' docs/`
    returns the doc(s) covering all five.
- [ ] The moved content is semantically complete vs. the current `CLAUDE.md` sections (reviewer
  confirms via side-by-side diff in the change description; no rule dropped).
- [ ] `docs/README.md` index references the new/updated doc(s).
  - Proving: `rg -n 'ticket-model|idea status|tags' docs/README.md`
- [ ] `make lint` passes (docs-only change; no code touched).

## De-risking results (content-model analysis — corrects the premise)

A grounded read of `docs/` shows the "five topics with no backing doc" premise is partly wrong.
Three of the five already have authoritative *write-level* homes. The real gap is one
**agent-facing conceptual home** for the ticket model. Refined plan:

- **New `docs/ticket-model.md`** holds the *concept / agent-facing surface* for FOUR topics:
  (a) idea-status lifecycle (its dispatch/gate/`validate` semantics are genuinely unhomed today),
  (b) hierarchy/containment (`parent_id`, not a link relation), (c) linking (the six relations +
  the promotion *concept*), (d) the tags surface + the `--set-tags` add-wins convergence caveat.
  **Cross-reference `docs/event-schema.md` for the write-level event mechanics (CREATE genesis,
  LINK/UNLINK, TAG_DELTA) — do NOT restate event bodies (drift risk).**
- **Parent-first cascade: NOT new content.** `docs/concurrency.md` I4a (~lines 97-153) already
  documents it *more completely* than CLAUDE.md (adds race-ownership, `STATUS_FORK_RESOLVED`,
  code cites). The one missing piece is CLAUDE.md's **gate-interaction paragraph** (a cascaded
  parent claim runs the parent's own plan-review gate; epics/stories are not gate-exempt;
  `--force` propagates) → **fold that single paragraph into concurrency.md I4a**; AGENTS.md
  points at I4a.
- **Session logs: split, don't lump.** Fold the *type semantics* into `docs/event-schema.md`
  under a new `## The session_log ticket type` heading (promote the existing CREATE-row note);
  keep the *capture helper + auto-rotation* in `docs/user-guide.md` (its Session-logs section
  already exists), cross-referencing `docs/config.md` for the fingerprint resolver.

Revised Acceptance Criteria (supersede the generic ones above):
- [ ] `docs/ticket-model.md` exists and covers idea-status, hierarchy/containment, linking
  (relations+promotion concept), and tags surface+`--set-tags` caveat, with cross-refs to
  event-schema.md and no duplicated event bodies.
  - Proving: `test -f docs/ticket-model.md && rg -c 'idea|parent_id|discovered_from|set-tags' docs/ticket-model.md`
- [ ] `docs/concurrency.md` I4a gains CLAUDE.md's cascade gate-interaction nuance.
  - Proving: `rg -n 'gate-exempt|plan-review|--force' docs/concurrency.md`
- [ ] `docs/event-schema.md` gains a `## The session_log ticket type` section; user-guide.md
  retains the capture helper.
- [ ] `docs/README.md` indexes `docs/ticket-model.md`.
- [ ] `make lint` passes.
